evals.prompt: Add a Supplier model to sabsys with full CRUD stack

analyst_observations: Both configurations scored 15/15 on all assertions. This is expected — the baseline prompt included a detailed architecture summary that covers all the patterns. In a real-world usage, the baseline would lack that context entirely.
The with_skill output produced 17 files (including more template files and apps.py) vs 8 for the baseline, showing the skill encourages more complete scaffolding.

Key qualitative differences: with_skill generated Spanish URL path segments (/editar/, /eliminar/), a separate SupplierCreateView, a supplier_detail.html template, and more thorough test coverage (11 tests vs 14 split across 5 classes).

The assertions are good coverage tests but not discriminating enough when the baseline prompt mirrors the skill's content. In a real usage scenario without the architecture context, the gap would be more visible.
Both outputs are high quality and copy-pasteable. The skill is ready to package.
